about.title: Connecting renters to the entire real estate market — without barriers
about.subtitle: GlobalRent bridges the gap between tenants and property owners, agencies, and real estate professionals across France, Tunisia, Egypt and Canada — all in one multilingual platform.
about.missionTitle: One platform. All the market. No language barrier.
about.missionDesc1: Finding a rental property in a foreign country is hard. You face scattered listings across dozens of local platforms, language differences, unfamiliar legal systems, and a lack of transparency.
about.missionDesc2: GlobalRent solves this by aggregating the best listings from the top real estate platforms in each country, translating them into {count} languages, and presenting them in a single, easy-to-use interface.
about.whoWeConnectDesc: GlobalRent acts as the bridge between all actors of the real estate rental market
about.multilingualDesc1: One of the biggest obstacles for international renters is the language barrier.
about.multilingualDesc2: Every listing on GlobalRent is automatically translated so you always understand what you're reading.
about.startExploringDesc: Browse thousands of verified rentals across 4 countries, in your language.
about.actor1Desc: Private landlords who list their property directly — no intermediary, direct contact with the tenant.
about.actor2Desc: Professional agencies managing multiple properties across cities and countries, featured prominently on our platform.
about.actor3Desc: Property managers, developers, and real estate professionals who want to reach a wider, international audience.
about.val1Desc: Everyone deserves access to a fair rental market, regardless of their language or location.
about.val2Desc: We show real listings from verified sources with accurate information and no hidden fees.
about.val3Desc: Find the right property faster by searching across all platforms at once, from one place.
about.val4Desc: We bring tenants, landlords, agencies and professionals together in a seamless experience.

contact.title: We're here to help
contact.subtitle: Have a question or need assistance? Reach out to the GlobalRent team — we usually respond within 24 hours.

contact.successMessage: Your message has been sent! We'll get back to you within 24 hours.
contact.errorMessage: Something went wrong. Please try again or email us directly.

tips.heroTitle: Tips for renting with peace of mind
tips.heroSubtitle: Whether you're looking for your first apartment or you're an experienced tenant, find everything you need to know before signing a lease.
tips.tipsSubtitle: Best practices to adopt before, during and after your rental
tips.typesSubtitle: Each type of rental has its own rules and advantages
tips.legalSubtitle: Master the vocabulary to better understand your rights and obligations
tips.ctaTitle: Ready to find your next home?
tips.ctaDesc: Browse thousands of verified listings in 4 countries.
tips.tip1Desc: Visit the property in person or via video, check the condition of equipment, meters, and the general state of the place.
tips.tip2Desc: Never sign a lease without reading it in full. Pay attention to termination clauses, charges amount, and renewal conditions.
tips.tip3Desc: The rent is not the only expense. Factor in charges, housing tax, insurance, and any agency fees.
tips.tip4Desc: At move-in and move-out, photograph every room and note any imperfections to avoid disputes over the security deposit.
tips.tip5Desc: Legislation varies by country. Learn about notice periods, rent increase rules, and your remedies in case of a dispute.
tips.tip6Desc: Maintain a relationship of trust with your landlord. Report any breakdown or damage quickly to avoid future complications.
tips.type1Desc: The property is provided with all the furniture and equipment needed for daily life.
tips.type1Point1: Ideal for short or temporary stays
tips.type1Point2: Rent is generally higher than unfurnished
tips.type1Point3: More flexible lease (1 year renewable, 9 months for students)
tips.type2Desc: The property comes without furniture. The tenant brings their own furniture and equipment.
tips.type3Desc: Temporary rental for vacations or business travel, for a limited period.
tips.type3Point3: Rate per night or per week
tips.type4Desc: Lease intended for professional or commercial use, distinct from residential lease.
tips.type4Point1: Commercial lease of at least 9 years
tips.type4Point3: Subject to VAT depending on the activity
tips.legal1Def: Written contract between the landlord and tenant defining the rental conditions: duration, rent, charges, obligations of each party.
tips.legal2Def: Sum paid by the tenant at move-in, refunded at move-out after deduction of any withholdings for damages.
tips.legal3Def: Mandatory period to respect before leaving a property or requesting departure. Varies from 1 to 3 months depending on the country and type of lease.
tips.legal4Def: Expenses related to the use of the building (maintenance of common areas, water, collective heating...) recoverable by the landlord from the tenant.
tips.legal5Def: Document produced at move-in and move-out, describing the state of each room. It protects both tenant and landlord in case of a dispute.
tips.legal6Def: Index published quarterly allowing legal revision of the rent amount during the lease.
tips.legal7Def: Receipt issued by the landlord confirming rent payment. May be required by certain organizations (banks, administrations).
tips.legal8Def: Natural or legal person who agrees to pay rent in place of the tenant in case of default. Often required for young tenants.
tips.legal9Def: Clause in the lease providing for its automatic termination in case of serious breach by the tenant (unpaid rent, neighborhood disturbances).
